disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Heavy continuous bleeding from one nostril for ~45 minutes, soaking through three towels, not slowed by pinching
trigger_question_examples: How long has the nose been bleeding?
How much blood has there been?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's been about 45 minutes and it won't stop — he's soaked through three thick towels and pinching his nose did nothing.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: New pallor, lethargy and dizziness on sitting up over the last 15 minutes (features of significant blood loss)
trigger_question_examples: How is he in himself — is he pale, sleepy or dizzy?
Does he feel faint or lightheaded when he sits up?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: In the last 15 minutes he's gone really pale and floppy, and he says the room spins when he tries to sit up.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Known inherited coagulation-factor deficiency (Factor XI) associated with his genetic syndrome — impaired ability to form a stable clot
trigger_question_examples: Does he have any medical conditions or bleeding problems?
Is there a reason his blood might not clot well?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yes — he has a genetic condition and the doctors told us he's missing a clotting factor, so his blood doesn't clot properly.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Blood trickling down the posterior pharynx which the child keeps swallowing (aspiration / haematemesis risk)
trigger_question_examples: Is any blood going down the back of his throat?
Is he swallowing or coughing on blood?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yes, some blood is running down the back of his throat and he keeps swallowing it.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Standard first aid already attempted and failed — parents pinched the bony bridge of the nose rather than the soft part
trigger_question_examples: What have you already tried to stop the bleeding?
Where exactly did you pinch his nose?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: We pinched the hard bridge at the top of his nose for a while but it didn't help at all.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The home haemostatic medicine previously prescribed (topical tranexamic acid) is not available at home right now
trigger_question_examples: Do you have any medicines at home for his bleeds?
Were you given anything to use for a nosebleed?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: We were given a nose medicine for bleeds before but we've run out — we don't have any here.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Known congenital heart lesion (pulmonary valve stenosis) with a systolic murmur — reduced cardiac reserve if hypovolaemia worsens
trigger_question_examples: Does he have any heart conditions?
Any known heart murmur or valve problem?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: He has a narrowing of one of his heart valves and a murmur that the heart doctors follow.
